block_00: How can the shopping season affect stocks?
block_01: Black Friday, which is the day that follows Thanksgiving, is considered the beginning of the Christmas shopping season in the USA. The “world consumption” day is celebrated with huge discounts and special offers from retailers, which attract a massive crowd to their stores (or, taking the pandemic into account, to their websites).
block_02: Retailers are considered to be the primary beneficiaries of Black Friday, and their gains may be translated into their stock prices, which is why traders and investors keep their eyes peeled during this time. Learn which companies might do well during the holiday season and why.
block_03: Check the full article to find out more.
